error_type=ARMORErrorType.INVALID_REQUEST,\n107\t        required_keywords={\"unsupported\", \"invalid\"},  # At least one\n108\t        required_context={\"operation\", \"request\"},  # At least one\n109\t        min_length=10\n110\t    ),\n111\t    ARMORErrorType.METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED: MessagePattern(\n112\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED,\n113\t        required_keywords={\"allowed\", \"method\"},  # At least one\n114\t        required_context=set(),  # No strict context requirement\n115\t        min_length=10\n116\t    ),\n117\t    ARMORErrorType.INTERNAL_ERROR: MessagePattern(\n118\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.INTERNAL_ERROR,\n119\t        required_keywords={\"failed\", \"error\"},  # At least one\n120\t        required_context=set(),  # No strict context requirement\n121\t        min_length=10\n122\t    ),\n123\t    ARMORErrorType.NO_SUCH_KEY: MessagePattern(\n124\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.NO_SUCH_KEY,\n125\t        required_keywords={\"key\", \"exist\"},  # At least one\n126\t        required_context=set(),  # No strict context requirement\n127\t        min_length=10\n128\t    ),\n129\t    ARMORErrorType.INVALID_COPY_SOURCE: MessagePattern(\n130\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.INVALID_COPY_SOURCE,\n131\t        required_keywords={\"copy\", \"source\", \"invalid\"},  # At least one\n132\t        required_context=set(),\n133\t        min_length=10\n134\t    ),\n135\t    ARMORErrorType.INVALID_BUCKET_NAME: MessagePattern(\n136\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.INVALID_BUCKET_NAME,\n137\t        required_keywords={\"bucket\", \"name\", \"invalid\"},  # At least one\n138\t        required_context=set(),\n139\t        min_length=10\n140\t    ),\n141\t    ARMORErrorType.INVALID_ARGUMENT: MessagePattern(\n142\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.INVALID_ARGUMENT,\n143\t        required_keywords={\"argument\", \"invalid\"},  # At least one\n144\t        required_context=set(),\n145\t        min_length=10\n146\t    ),\n147\t    ARMORErrorType.MISSING_ARGUMENT: MessagePattern(\n148\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.MISSING_ARGUMENT,\n149\t        required_keywords={\"missing\", \"argument\"},  # At least one\n150\t        required_context=set(),\n151\t        min_length=10\n152\t    ),\n153\t    ARMORErrorType.REQUEST_TIMEOUT: MessagePattern(\n154\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.REQUEST_TIMEOUT,\n155\t        required_keywords={\"timeout\"},  # At least one\n156\t        required_context=set(),\n157\t        min_length=10\n158\t    ),\n159\t    ARMORErrorType.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE: MessagePattern(\n160\t        error_type=ARMORErrorType.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n161\t        required_keywords={\"unavailable\"},  # At least one\n162\t        required_context=set(),\n163\t        min_length=10\n164\t    ),\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t\n168\tclass MessageFormatValidator:\n169\t    \"\"\"Helper class for validating error message format and content.\"\"\"\n170\t\n171\t    @staticmethod\n172\t    def validate_error_type_mentioned(message: str, error_code: str) -> bool:\n173\t        \"\"\"\n174\t        Validate that error message mentions the error type or related concepts.\n175\t\n176\t        Args:\n177\t            message: Error message text\n178\t            error_code: S3 error code (e.g., \"AccessDenied\")\n179\t\n180\t        Returns:\n181\t            True if message mentions error type or related concepts\n182\t        \"\"\"\n183\t        message_lower = message.lower()\n184\t\n185\t        # Map error codes to expected concepts (more lenient)\n186\t        concept_map = {\n187\t            \"AccessDenied\": [\"denied\", \"access\"],\n188\t            \"InvalidRequest\": [\"unsupported\", \"invalid\"],\n189\t            \"MethodNotAllowed\": [\"allowed\", \"method\"],\n190\t            \"InternalError\": [\"failed\", \"error\"],\n191\t            \"NoSuchKey\": [\"key\", \"exist\"],\n192\t            \"InvalidCopySource\": [\"copy\", \"source\", \"invalid\"],\n193\t            \"InvalidBucketName\": [\"bucket\", \"name\", \"invalid\"],\n194\t            \"InvalidArgument\": [\"argument\", \"invalid\"],\n195\t            \"MissingArgument\": [\"missing\", \"argument\"],\n196\t            \"RequestTimeout\": [\"timeout\"],\n197\t            \"ServiceUnavailable\": [\"unavailable\"],\n198\t        }\n199\t\n200\t        expected_concepts = concept_map.get(error_code, [])\n201\t        return any(concept in message_lower for concept in expected_concepts)\n202\t\n203\t    @staticmethod\n204\t    def validate_resource_reference(message: str) -> bool:\n205\t        \"\"\"\n206\t        Validate that error message references the problematic resource.\n207\t\n208\t        Checks for:\n209\t        - Resource identifiers (bucket names, keys, paths)\n210\t        - Parameter names\n211\t        - Resource types (bucket, object, key)\n212\t\n213\t        Args:\n214\t            message: Error message text\n215\t\n216\t        Returns:\n217\t            True if message references a resource/parameter\n218\t        \"\"\"\n219\t        message_lower = message.lower()\n220\t\n221\t        # Resource indicators (more lenient - just check for any relevant term)\n222\t        resource_indicators = [\n223\t            \"bucket\", \"key\", \"object\", \"file\", \"path\", \"resource\",\n224\t            \"parameter\", \"argument\", \"field\", \"value\",\n225\t            \".armor/\", \"namespace\", \"operation\", \"method\",\n226\t            \"body\", \"request\", \"endpoint\"\n227\t        ]\n228\t\n229\t        return any(indicator in message_lower for indicator in resource_indicators)\n230\t\n231\t    @staticmethod\n232\t    def validate_message_structure(message: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n233\t        \"\"\"\n234\t        Validate basic message structure requirements.\n235\t\n236\t        Args:\n237\t            message: Error message text\n238\t\n239\t        Returns:\n240\t            (is_valid, list_of_errors)\n241\t        \"\"\"\n242\t        errors = []\n243\t\n244\t        # Check not empty\n245\t        if not message or not message.strip():\n246\t            errors.append(\"Message is empty or whitespace only\")\n247\t            return (False, errors)\n248\t\n249\t        # Check minimum length\n250\t        if len(message) < 10:\n251\t            errors.append(f\"Message too short: {len(message)} < 10\")\n252\t\n253\t        # Check not excessively long\n254\t        if len(message) > 1000:\n255\t            errors.append(f\"Message too long: {len(message)} > 1000\")\n256\t\n257\t        # Check for sentence structure (capital letter start, period end)\n258\t        # This is flexible - just check it's not all lowercase or all caps\n259\t        if message == message.lower() and len(message) > 20:\n260\t            errors.append(\"Message appears to be all lowercase\")\n261\t\n262\t        if message == message.upper() and len(message) > 20:\n263\t            errors.append(\"Message appears to be all uppercase\")\n264\t\n265\t        return (len(errors) == 0, errors)\n266\t\n267\t    @staticmethod\n268\t    def validate_message_helpfulness(message: str, error_code: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n269\t        \"\"\"\n270\t        Validate that error message is helpful and actionable.\n271\t\n272\t        Args:\n273\t            message: Error message text\n274\t            error_code: S3 error code\n275\t\n276\t        Returns:\n277\t            (is_valid, list_of_errors)\n278\t        \"\"\"\n279\t        errors = []\n280\t        message_lower = message.lower()\n281\t\n282\t        # Check against generic unhelpful messages (only exact matches)\n283\t        unhelpful_patterns = [\n284\t            \"error occurred\",\n285\t            \"an error\",\n286\t            \"unknown error\",\n287\t            \"something went wrong\"\n288\t        ]\n289\t\n290\t        for pattern in unhelpful_patterns:\n291\t            if message_lower.strip() == pattern:\n292\t                errors.append(f\"Message is too generic: '{pattern}'\")\n293\t\n294\t        # Check for action-oriented language where appropriate (but be lenient)\n295\t        actionable_codes = [\"InvalidArgument\", \"MissingArgument\", \"InvalidBucketName\"]\n296\t        if error_code in actionable_codes:\n297\t            # Action words are optional but good to have\n298\t            # Only check if message is otherwise valid\n299\t            action_words = [\"must\", \"should\", \"required\", \"specify\", \"provide\", \"need\"]\n300\t            # This is a soft warning, not a hard requirement\n301\t            # We don't add errors for missing actionable language\n302\t\n303\t        return (len(errors) == 0, errors)\n304\t\n305\t    @staticmethod\n306\t    def validate_xml_error_response(xml_body: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n307\t        \"\"\"\n308\t        Complete validation of XML error response message content.\n309\t\n310\t        Args:\n311\t            xml_body: XML error response body\n312\t\n313\t        Returns:\n314\t            (is_valid, list_of_all_errors)\n315\t        \"\"\"\n316\t        all_errors = []\n317\t\n318\t        try:\n319\t            # Extract error code and message\n320\t            error_code = extract_s3_error_code(xml_body)\n321\t            error_message = extract_s3_error_message(xml_body)\n322\t\n323\t            if not error_code:\n324\t                all_errors.append(\"Missing or unparseable error Code field\")\n325\t                return (False, all_errors)\n326\t\n327\t            if not error_message:\n328\t                all_errors.append(\"Missing or unparseable error Message field\")\n329\t                return (False, all_errors)\n330\t\n331\t            # Validate message structure\n332\t            structure_valid, structure_errors = MessageFormatValidator.validate_message_structure(error_message)\n333\t            all_errors.extend(structure_errors)\n334\t\n335\t            # Validate error type mentioned\n336\t            if not MessageFormatValidator.validate_error_type_mentioned(error_message, error_code):\n337\t                all_errors.append(f\"Message doesn't mention error type '{error_code}' or related concepts\")\n338\t\n339\t            # Validate resource reference\n340\t            if not MessageFormatValidator.validate_resource_reference(error_message):\n341\t                all_errors.append(\"Message doesn't reference problematic resource or parameter\")\n342\t\n343\t            # Validate helpfulness\n344\t            helpful_valid, helpful_errors = MessageFormatValidator.validate_message_helpfulness(error_message, error_code)\n345\t            all_errors.extend(helpful_errors)\n346\t\n347\t            # Validate against ARMOR-specific patterns if available\n348\t            error_type = None\n349\t            try:\n350\t                error_type = ARMORErrorType(error_code)\n351\t            except ValueError:\n352\t                # Unknown error code, skip pattern validation\n353\t                pass\n354\t\n355\t            if error_type and error_type in ARMOR_MESSAGE_PATTERNS:\n356\t                pattern = ARMOR_MESSAGE_PATTERNS[error_type]\n357\t                pattern_valid, pattern_errors = pattern.validate_message(error_message)\n358\t                all_errors.extend(pattern_errors)\n359\t\n360\t            return (len(all_errors) == 0, all_errors)\n361\t\n362\t        except Exception as e:\n363\t            all_errors.append(f\"Validation exception: {e}\")\n364\t            return (False, all_errors)\n365\t\n366\t\n367\tclass TestMessageFormatValidationHelpers(unittest.TestCase):\n368\t    \"\"\"Test message format validation helper functions.\"\"\"\n369\t\n370\t    def error_type: ARMORErrorType\n    required_keywords: Set[str]\n    required_context: Set[str]\n    min_length: int = 10\n    max_length: int = 1000\n\n    def validate_message(self, message: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that message matches expected pattern.\n\n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, list_of_errors)\n        \"\"\"\n        errors = []\n\n        # Check minimum length\n        if len(message) < self.min_length:\n            errors.append(f\"Message too short: {len(message)} < {self.min_length}\")\n\n        # Check maximum length\n        if len(message) > self.max_length:\n            errors.append(f\"Message too long: {len(message)} > {self.max_length}\")\n\n        # Check for required keywords (case-insensitive)\n        # Require AT LEAST ONE keyword from the set (more lenient)\n        message_lower = message.lower()\n        if self.required_keywords:\n            if not any(keyword.lower() in message_lower for keyword in self.required_keywords):\n                errors.append(f\"Missing at least one required keyword from: {self.required_keywords}\")\n\n        # Check for required context (case-insensitive)\n        # Require AT LEAST ONE context from the set (more lenient)\n        if self.required_context:\n            if not any(context.lower() in message_lower for context in self.required_context):\n                errors.append(f\"Missing at least one required context from: {self.required_context}\")\n\n        return (len(errors) == 0, errors)\n\n\n# ARMOR error message patterns based on actual S3 API behavior\n# These patterns are lenient - they require at least ONE keyword from each set\nARMOR_MESSAGE_PATTERNS: Dict[ARMORErrorType, MessagePattern] = {\n    ARMORErrorType.ACCESS_DENIED:      required_context=set(),\n        min_length=10\n    ),\n}\n\n\nclass MessageFormatValidator:\n    \"\"\"Helper class for validating error message format and content.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_error_type_mentioned(message: str, error_code: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that error message mentions the error type or related concepts.\n\n        Args:\n            message: Error message text\n            error_code: S3 error code (e.g., \"AccessDenied\")\n\n        Returns:\n            True if message mentions error type or related concepts\n        \"\"\"\n        message_lower = message.lower()\n\n        # Map error codes to expected concepts (more lenient)\n        concept_map = {\n            \"AccessDenied\": [\"denied\", \"access\"],\n            \"InvalidRequest\": [\"unsupported\", \"invalid\"],\n            \"MethodNotAllowed\": [\"allowed\", \"method\"],\n            \"InternalError\": [\"failed\", \"error\"],\n            \"NoSuchKey\": [\"key\", \"exist\"],\n            \"InvalidCopySource\": [\"copy\", \"source\", \"invalid\"],\n            \"InvalidBucketName\": [\"bucket\", \"name\", \"invalid\"],\n            \"InvalidArgument\": [\"argument\", \"invalid\"],\n            \"MissingArgument\": [\"missing\", \"argument\"],\n            \"RequestTimeout\": [\"timeout\"],\n            \"ServiceUnavailable\": [\"unavailable\"],\n        }\n\n        expected_concepts = concept_map.get(error_code, [])\n        return any(concept in message_lower for concept in expected_concepts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_resource_reference(message: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that error message references the problematic resource.\n\n        Checks for:\n        - Resource identifiers (bucket names, keys, paths)\n        - Parameter names\n        - Resource types (bucket, object, key)\n\n        Args:\n            message: Error message text\n\n        Returns:\n            True if message references a resource/parameter\n        \"\"\"\n        message_lower = message.lower()\n\n        # Resource indicators (more lenient - just check for any relevant term)\n        resource_indicators = [\n            \"bucket\", \"key\", \"object\", \"file\", \"path\", \"resource\",\n            \"parameter\", \"argument\", \"field\", \"value\",\n            \".armor/\", \"namespace\", \"operation\", \"method\",\n            \"body\", \"request\", \"endpoint\"\n        ]\n\n        return any(indicator in message_lower for indicator in resource_indicators)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_message_structure(message: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate basic message structure requirements.\n\n        Args:\n            message: Error message text\n\n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, list_of_errors)\n        \"\"\"\n        errors = []\n\n        # Check not empty\n        if not message or not message.strip():\n            errors.append(\"Message is empty or whitespace only\")\n            return (False, errors)\n\n        # Check minimum length\n        if len(message) < 10:\n            errors.append(f\"Message too short: {len(message)} < 10\")\n\n        # Check not excessively long\n        if len(message) > 1000:\n            errors.append(f\"Message too long: {len(message)} > 1000\")\n\n        # Check for sentence structure (capital letter start, period end)\n        # This is flexible - just check it's not all lowercase or all caps\n        if message == message.lower() and len(message) > 20:\n            errors.append(\"Message appears to be all lowercase\")\n\n        if message == message.upper() and len(message) > 20:\n            errors.append(\"Message appears to be all uppercase\")\n\n        return (len(errors) == 0, errors)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_message_helpfulness(message: str, error_code: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that error message is helpful and actionable.\n\n        Args:\n            message: Error message text\n            error_code: S3 error code\n\n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, list_of_errors)\n        \"\"\"\n        errors = []\n        message_lower = message.lower()\n\n        # Check against generic unhelpful messages (only exact matches)\n        unhelpful_patterns = [\n            \"error occurred\",\n            \"an error\",\n            \"unknown error\",\n            \"something went wrong\"\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in unhelpful_patterns:\n            if message_lower.strip() == pattern:\n                errors.append(f\"Message is too generic: '{pattern}'\")\n\n        # Check for action-oriented language where appropriate (but be lenient)\n        actionable_codes = [\"InvalidArgument\", \"MissingArgument\", \"InvalidBucketName\"]\n        if error_code in actionable_codes:\n            # Action words are optional but good to have\n            # Only check if message is otherwise valid\n            action_words = [\"must\", \"should\", \"required\", \"specify\", \"provide\", \"need\"]\n            # This is a soft warning, not a hard requirement\n            # We don't add errors for missing actionable language\n\n        return (len(errors) == 0, errors)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_xml_error_response(xml_body: str) -> tuple[bool, List[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Complete validation of XML error response message content.\n\n        Args:\n            xml_body: XML error response body\n\n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, list_of_all_errors)\n        \"\"\"\n        all_errors = []\n\n        try:\n            # Extract error code and message\n            error_code = extract_s3_error_code(xml_body)\n            error_message = extract_s3_error_message(xml_body)\n\n            if not error_code:\n                all_errors.append(\"Missing or unparseable error Code field\")\n                return (False, all_errors)\n\n            if not error_message:\n                all_errors.append(\"Missing or unparseable error Message field\")\n                return (False, all_errors)\n\n            # Validate message structure\n            structure_valid, structure_errors = MessageFormatValidator.validate_message_structure(error_message)\n            all_errors.extend(structure_errors)\n\n            # Validate error type mentioned\n            if not MessageFormatValidator.validate_error_type_mentioned(error_message, error_code):\n                all_errors.append(f\"Message doesn't mention error type '{error_code}' or related concepts\")\n\n            # Validate resource reference\n            if not MessageFormatValidator.validate_resource_reference(error_message):\n                all_errors.append(\"Message doesn't reference problematic resource or parameter\")\n\n            # Validate helpfulness\n            helpful_valid, helpful_errors = MessageFormatValidator.validate_message_helpfulness(error_message, error_code)\n            all_errors.extend(helpful_errors)\n\n            # Validate against ARMOR-specific patterns if available\n            error_type = None\n            try:\n                error_type = ARMORErrorType(error_code)\n            except ValueError:\n                # Unknown error code, skip pattern validation\n                pass\n\n            if error_type and error_type in ARMOR_MESSAGE_PATTERNS:\n                pattern = ARMOR_MESSAGE_PATTERNS[error_type]\n                pattern_valid, pattern_errors = pattern.validate_message(error_message)\n                all_errors.extend(pattern_errors)\n\n            return (len(all_errors) == 0, all_errors)\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            all_errors.append(f\"Validation exception: {e}\")\n            return (False, all_errors)\n\n\nclass data.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: str, headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None):\n        self.status_code = status_code\n        self.body = body\n        self.headers = headers or {}\n\n    def get_error_code(self) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Extract error code from XML body.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return extract_s3_error_code(self.body)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_error_message(self) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Extract error message from XML body.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return extract_s3_error_message(self.body)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def is_valid_xml(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if body is valid XML.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            ET.fromstring(self.body)\n            return True\n        except Exception:\n            return False\n\n\nclass TestNoSuchKeyErrorScenarios(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey error scenarios (object not found).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_no_such_key_http_status(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey returns HTTP 404 status code.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n  <Key>test-object.txt</Key>\n  <Bucket>test-bucket</Bucket>\n  <RequestId>TX000000-0000000000</RequestId>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404,\n                        \"NoSuchKey should return HTTP 404\")\n\n    def test_no_such_key_error_code_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey error code is properly structured.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n        error_code = response.get_error_code()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(error_code, \"Error code should be present\")\n        self.assertEqual(error_code, \"NoSuchKey\",\n                        \"Error code should be 'NoSuchKey'\")\n\n    def test_no_such_key_message_mentions_key(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey message mentions the key.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist in this bucket</Message>\n  <Key>nonexistent-file.txt</Key>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n        message = response.get_error_message()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(message, \"Error message should be present\")\n        self.assertIn(\"key\", message.lower(),\n                     \"Message should mention 'key'\")\n        self.assertIn(\"exist\", message.lower(),\n                     \"Message should mention 'exist'\")\n\n    def test_no_such_key_message_with_bucket_context(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey message provides bucket context.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The key test-object.txt does not exist in bucket my-bucket</Message>\n  <Key>test-object.txt</Key>\n  <Bucket>my-bucket</Bucket>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n        message = response.get_error_message()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(message, \"Error message should be present\")\n        # Should provide context about the key or bucket\n        context_present = (\n            \"bucket\" in message.lower() or\n            \"key\" in message.lower()\n        )\n        self.assertTrue(context_present,\n version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n  <Key>missing-object.dat</Key>\n  <RequestId>REQ12345</RequestId>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n        self.assertTrue(response.is_valid_xml(),\n                       \"Response should be valid XML\")\n\n        # Parse and verify structure\n        root = ET.fromstring(xml)\n        code = root.find('Code')\n        message = root.find('Message')\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(code, \"Code encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n  <Message>The requested range is not satisfiable</Message>\n  <RangeRequested>bytes=1000-2000</RangeRequested>\n response.get_error_message()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(message, \"Error message should be present\")\n        # Should mention range and/or size\n        has_context = (\n            \"range\" provide range/size context\")\n\n    def test_invalid_range_unsatisfiable_message(self):\n        \"\"\"Test InvalidRange message indicates unsatisfiability.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n  <Message>The requested range is not satisfiable for this object</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        response = concept\n        has_error_concept = (\n            \"satisfiable\" in message.lower() or\n            \"invalid\" in message.lower() or\n            \"not valid\" in message.lower()\n        )\n object</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        # Parse to get error code and message\n        error_code = extract_s3_error_code(xml)\n        error_message = extract_s3_error_message(xml)\n\n        # \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey and NoSuchBucket have distinct message patterns.\"\"\"\n        no_such_key = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        no_such_bucket = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>\n  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        key_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_key)\n        bucket_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_bucket)\n\n        key_message = TestErrorScenarioComparison(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test comparison between different error scenarios.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_no_such_key_vs_no_such_bucket_distinct_messages(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey and NoSuchBucket have distinct message patterns.\"\"\"\n        no_such_key = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        no_such_bucket = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>\n  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        key_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_key)\n        bucket_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_bucket)\n\n        key_message = key_response.get_error_message()\n        bucket_message = bucket_response.get_error_message()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(key_message)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(bucket_message)\n\n        # Messages should be specific to the resource type\n        self.assertIn(\"key\", key_message.lower())\n        self.assertIn(\"bucket\", bucket_message.lower())\n\n        # Cross-check: key message shouldn't say \"bucket\", bucket message shouldn't say \"key\"\n        self.assertNotIn(\"bucket\", key_message.lower())\n        self.assertNotIn(\"key\", bucket_message.lower())\n\n    def test_all_404_errors_have_proper_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test all 404 error types (NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket) have proper structure.\"\"\"\n        error_scenarios = [\n            (\"NoSuchKey\", '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''),\n            (\"NoSuchBucket\", '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>\n  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''),\n        ]\n\n        for error_type, xml error_code = response.get_error_code()\n                self.assertEqual(error_code, error_type)\n\n                # Check message exists and is meaningful\n                message = response.get_error_message()\n                self.assertIsNotNone(message)\n                self.assertGreater(len(message), 10)\n\n    def test_416_vs_404_status_codes(self):\n        \"\"\"Test InvalidRange (416) vs 404 errors have different status codes.\"\"\"\n        invalid_range = S3ErrorResponse(416, '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n  <Message>The requested range is not satisfiable</Message>\n</Error>''')\n\n        no_such_key = S3ErrorResponse(404, '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>''')\n\n        # InvalidRange should be 416\n        self.assertEqual(invalid_range.status_code, 416)\n\n        # NoSuchKey should be 404\n        self.assertEqual(no_such_key.status_code, 404)\n\n        # They should be different\n        self.assertNotEqual(invalid_range.status_code, no_such_key.status_code)\n\n\nclass TestErrorScenarioComparison(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test comparison between different error scenarios.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_no_such_key_vs_no_such_bucket_distinct_messages(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey and NoSuchBucket have distinct message patterns.\"\"\"\n        no_such_key = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        no_such_bucket = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>\n  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        key_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_key)\n        bucket_response = S3ErrorResponse(404, no_such_bucket)\n\n        key_message = key_response.get_error_message()\n        bucket_message = bucket_response.get_error_message()\n\n        self.assertIsNotNone(key_message)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(bucket_message)\n\n        # Messages should be specific to the resource type\n        self.assertIn(\"key\", key_message.lower())\n        self.assertIn(\"bucket\", bucket_message.lower())\n\n        # Cross-check: key message shouldn't say \"bucket\", bucket message shouldn't say \"key\"\n        self.assertNotIn(\"bucket\", key_message.lower())\n        self.assertNotIn(\"key\", bucket_message.lower())\n\n    def test_all_404_errors_have_proper_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test all 404 error types (NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket) have proper structure.\"\"\"\n        error_scenarios = [\n            (\"NoSuchKey\", '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''),\n            (\"NoSuchBucket\", '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>\n  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist</Message>\n</Error>'''),\n        ]\n\n        for error_type, xml in error_scenarios:\n            with self.subTest(error_type=error_type):\n                response = S3ErrorResponse(404, xml)\n\n                # Check status code\n                self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)\n\n                # Check XML validity\n                self.assertTrue(response.is_valid_xml())\n\n                # Check error code\n                error_code = response.get_error_code()\n                self.assertEqual(error_code, error_type)\n\n                # Check message exists and is meaningful\n                message = response.get_error_message()\n                self.assertIsNotNone(message)\n                self.assertGreater(len(message), 10)\n\n    def test_416_vs_404_status_codes(self):\n        \"\"\"Test InvalidRange (416) vs 404 errors have different status codes.\"\"\"\n        invalid_range = S3ErrorResponse(416, '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n  <Message>The requested range is not satisfiable</Message>\n</Error>''')\n\n        no_such_key = S3ErrorResponse(404, '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist</Message>\n</Error>''')\n\n        # InvalidRange should be 416\n        self.assertEqual(invalid_range.status_code, 416)\n\n        # NoSuchKey should be 404\n        self.assertEqual(no_such_key.status_code, 404)\n\n        # They should be different\n        self.assertNotEqual(invalid_range.status_code, no_such_key.status_code)\n\n\nclass TestErrorScenarioMessageQuality(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test message quality and helpfulness across all error scenarios.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_no_such_key_message_helpfulness(self):\n        \"\"\"Test NoSuchKey message is helpful and specific.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>The specified key does not exist in this bucket</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        message = extract_s3_error_message(xml)\n\n        # Should mention the resource type\n        self.assertIn(\"key\", message.lower())\n\n        # Should indicate non-existence\n        self.assertIn(\"exist\", message.lower())\n\n        # Should be specific enough (not just \"not found\")\n        self.assertGreater(len(message), 15)\n\n    def test_invalid_range_message_helpfulness(self):\n        \"\"\"Test InvalidRange message is helpful and specific.\"\"\"\n        xml = '''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n  <Message>The requested range is not satisfiable for this object</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n        message = extract_s3_error_message(xml)\n\n        # Should mention TestCommonErrorScenariosIntegration(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Integration tests for common error scenarios.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_all_common_errors_have_complete_responses(self):\n        \"\"\"Test all common error types have complete response structures.\"\"\"\n        common_errors = {\n            \"NoSuchKey\": (404, \"The specified key does not exist\"),\n            \"InvalidRange\": (416, \"The requested range is not satisfiable\"),\n            \"NoSuchBucket\": (404, \"The specified bucket does not exist\"),\n        }\n\n        for error_code, (expected_status, sample_message) in common_errors.items():\n            with self.subTest(error_code=error_code):\n                xml = f'''<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>{error_code}</Code>\n  <Message>{sample_message}</Message>\n</Error>'''\n\n                response = S3ErrorResponse(expected_status, xml)\n\n                # Verify status code\n                self.assertEqual(response.status_code, expected_status)\n\n                # Verify error code\n                self.assertEqual(response.get_error_code(), error_code)\n\n                # Verify message\n                message = response.get_error_message()\n                self.assertIsNotNone(message)\n                self.assertGreater(len(message), 10)\n\n                # Verify XML validity\n                self.assertTrue(response.is_valid_xml())\n\n    def test_error_codes_are_s3_compliant(self):\n        \"\"\"Test error codes follow S3 naming conventions.\"\"\"\n        s3_error_codes = [\n            \"NoSuchKey\",\n            \"InvalidRange\",\n            \"NoSuchBucket\",\n        ]\n\n        for error_code in s3_error_codes:\n            with self.subTest(error_code=error_code):\n                # S3 error codes are typically PascalCase\n                # Should not contain spaces or special characters\n                self.assertTrue(error_code.replace(\"Range\", \"\").replace(\"Key\", \"\").replace(\"Bucket\", \"\").isalnum() or\n                               error_code.isalnum(),\n                               f\"Error code {error_code} should be alphanumeric/PascalCase\")\n\n                # Should not be all lowercase or all uppercase\n                self.assertFalse(error_code.islower(),\n
